title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 10 - Caverns of Cynder Expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jfEMLlPzKEU

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes an honest look at the Caverns of Cynder, the first otherworld expansion to hit retail for Shadows of Brimstone — and his least favourite out of the box. The fiery dimension of lava and ash ships with only one enemy type (lava men), limited threat variety, and fewer components than its peers. However, it's also cheaper, and Daniel argues it becomes genuinely good once supplemented by the Belial extra-large boss expansion and the Hellfire Succubi mission pack.

The world rule "Intense Heat" punishes heroes for every piece of clothing equipped (one damage per item at activation), while granting +1 max grit. Lava spaces require agility checks to traverse safely, and lava men heal wounds when adjacent to lava — a neat environmental synergy. A rickety bridge tile nods to Warhammer Quest's instant-death bridges. The expansion introduces burning markers, hellfire markers, liquid dark stone, and modifier tokens for tracking stat changes.

Artifacts include a fire blade (d8 damage plus burning), robes granting burning immunity, and a fire parasite that boosts agility but inflicts corruption on movement rolls of six. Missions feature a bridge defense scenario Daniel particularly enjoys. He notes that Cynder is the only expansion he knows where "expansions expand expansions" — with Belial, Hellfire Succubi, and Forbidden Fortress crossover enemies eventually filling out its initially thin threat deck.

----

Have you ever had an expansion that felt underwhelming until other supplements transformed it?
